name: Mongolian Beef
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------------------SAUCE-----------------------------------
1 teaspoon chili paste with garlic
1 pinch sugar
1/4 cup chicken stock
1 1/2 tablespoons dry sherry
2 tablespoons dark soy sauce
+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
Preparation: Cut the tops of green onions into 2-inch long pieces. Combine the sauce ingredients in a small bowl and stir thoroughly. Cut steak across the grain into thin slices, about 1/2-inch deep by 2 inches long. In a large bowl, combine egg whites, salt, and water chestnut flour. Beat with a chopstick until frothy. Add steak, and use fingers to coat each slice.
Deep-frying: Heat oil in a wok to moderately hot. When ready, a piece of coated meat will rise to the surface immediately. Fry meat in small batches; drop one slice at a time to avoid sticking. Cook until lightly brown, about 1 minute. Drain on a Chinese strainer or paper bag.
Stir-frying: Remove all but 2 tablespoons oil from the wok. With the wok at medium heat, quickly stir-fry green onions and ginger for about 20 seconds. Add sauce; bring to boil on high heat while stirring. Add beef all at once, and toss with sauce until beef is hot and coated. Push beef out of the sauce, dribble in cornstarch paste to lightly thicken. Recombine. Serve immediately.
